100.description: The server has received the request headers and the client should proceed to send the request body.

201.description: The request has succeeded and a new resource has been created.

204.description: The server has successfully fulfilled the request and there is no additional content to send.

301.description: The requested resource has been assigned a new permanent URI.

400.description: The server cannot or will not process the request due to an apparent client error.

403.description: The server understood the request but refuses to authorize it.

404.description: The server can not find the requested resource.

429.description: The user has sent too many requests in a given amount of time.

500.description: The server encountered an unexpected condition that prevented it from fulfilling the request.

502.description: The server received an invalid response from the upstream server.

503.description: The server is currently unable to handle the request due to temporary overloading or maintenance.

504.description: The server did not receive a timely response from the upstream server.
